channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: VOO and Chill — 168 replies — 160.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
VOO and Chill

Open thread • Replies: 168 • Updated: 2026-07-01 12:53 UTC

OP

The more I look into investing and compare the historical returns of certain ETFs / individual stocks it seems like the whole philosophy of “VOO and Chill” is equal parts really smart and retarded
It’s like a no brainer for money but even now as long as the market is in a growth period shouldn’t you at least do QQQ?
It seems so simple that’d you miss a lot of upside but the returns are honestly not bad and extremely consistent
How accurate is this assessment? It’s both so simple and guaranteed that everyone should it but also too safe so you end up missing out on more upside
I’m talking 5-20 years investing here not trading

Early replies (first 3)

62400515
Boomer funds are gay and lame

62400554
>>62400515
What qualifies as one
Why
What crypto do you want to shill

62400560
boglehead method is the tried and tested way, buy the market with an index fund that has a low fee, invest monthly, do it consistently for decades and you WILL be rich
but biz/anons don't care about being rich when they're older, they want to be rich NOW
greed is a deadly sin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62423875
>>62400636
It actually is something tho. $150 a month becomes 170k at 7% real returns for 30 years. This gives you an extra $570/mo in retirement.

Is that a LOT? Fuck no. But we’re talking poorfag standards where social security is the only thing you have. An extra 500-600 a month is a significant increase over base SS for a poorfag especially if you move to an ultra low cost of living area.

62423880
The reason i keep bringing up DRAM and other return stuff is because it's on my mind to go into that stuff now, sell, and then increase my VOO position even further - not to infinitely chase returns btw

62424100
>>62423880
I'm putting 20k into micron tomorrow. I feel like it's not to late for this to just keep going up. Its not going to 10x but 4-5, yeah...

62424179
>>62424100
why only micron, why not dram etf

62424607
>>62400636
>The problem is we have people starting at age 35 with like 150 a month thinking it's going to somehow mean something.
I'm in this and doing VT. Maybe I'll be ok probably not. Can do more than $150 though

62424664
I seriously cannot decide between just not thinking and doing broad ETFs and trying to chase this AI thing

62426022
>>62423236
>>62423245
It's a mostly unserious backtest, site was posted earlier >>62419980

>>62423769
Why not have some fun and make the most unhinged etf port possible??

62426103
>>62426022
Interesting thanks

62426138
I have been degenerately long-only various semiconductors since the april bottom and yet on 90% of days, I've underperformed soxl on the way up and outperformed it on the way down. After doing all my research and picking the right names and calculating the right options to buy, I should have thrown all my notes in the trash and just bought soxl. Even if this is the bubble top and soxl is about to go down 90%, I know from experience my stock picks will go down 95%. So I stay may as well buy soxl.

62426144
If I'm just starting out with this stuff, what's wrong with 70% VTI / 30% VXUS?

62426154
Honestly I’m seeing a lot of upside to going into QQQ over VOO as long as you are okay with the fact it is more volatile
The upside it’s just so huge when compounded

62426173
>>62426154
Or honestly, as someone already in VOO, I wonder if it makes sense to just go into something like VGT which has consistently beaten VOO and QQQ (for now anyway) or something like DRAM
A lot of VGTs holdings are currently down but it’s YTD is higher

62426183
>>62407078
I keep half of my funds in VTWAX, rebalancing annually. Right now I'm down 0.84% this year, my portfolio is fucked but VTWAX is my safety net.

62427417
I am also curious about DRAM can anyone tell me if it’s a good hold for a short while

62428031
jfc DRAM (ie a thematic ETF) is bullshit made for retard retail investors that get their financial advice from grifters off of tiktok. this happens every time a finance topic hits the news and every time people get burned.
barely worth saying. if you're unironically taking advice from /biz/ you're already doomed intellectually.

62428054
>>62428031
but why exactly, what reason is there to believe the companies within it won't continue to turn a profit for the foreseeable future?

62428080
>>62428031
>>62428054
Like I'm serious, what exactly is wrong about holding it and selling once memory cools down, is it smarter to just go into a more volatile ETF that at least has a longer history if you want more returns, is it just hype and not really the next big thing, explain why

62428238
>>62428080
this is basic finance 101, there are plenty of resources online or off that will explain it to you.
ignorance isn't even the issue here, your reaction to not knowing something is to have random retards on a thinly veiled gambling board tell you what to think. and don't just jump on another grifter selling a course or ask some AI slop engine and ask it leading questions like "why is investing in DRAM a good idea?".

62428337
>>62428238
In other words you don't know

62429683
>>62400493
Anon there is nothing wrong with doing VOO and chill if you have time and principal
Going into wildly volatile ETFs can be okay if it’s a satellite account
Just be careful of FOMO and not being risk averse
You sound like you have a good head on your shoulders though
Remember this is money, real money, your money

62430693
VOO anons where you at?

62430902
>>62400493
Contrarian nerds always lose.
Always.
In middle school dating.
Being high school incels.
In college.
At work.
In love.
At investing.
At all times.
Follow the crowd and try to fit in. Everyone doing anything else is a reject loser.

62431131
>>62430902
Who is being the contrarian here though

62431374
VOO is managed by 2 girl bosses, Michelle Louie & Aurélie Denis. So with your 0.03% management fee you're essentially paying for those do-nothing "day in the life of a portfolio manager" videos for daughters of elite sociopaths.

62431402
>>62431374
A small price to pay for a low expense ratio

62431920
>>62431131
Anyone trying to beat VOO over decades.

62431922
>>62431920
Feel like VUG has consistently beaten VOO though

62431971
>>62431922
Contrarian.
I own VUG and don't own VOO tho :)

62432023
>>62431920
fair enough
thoughts on a younger person doing QQQM?

62434054
Anyone have thoughts or realized returns and experiences on VOO + satellite
accounts instead of just VOO

item.title: >kills millions by scrapping USAID >becomes trillionaire Why is it always the most evil people who become rich? — 29 replies — 108.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
>kills millions by scrapping USAID >becomes trillionaire Why is it always the most evil people who become rich?

Open thread • Replies: 29 • Updated: 2026-07-01 13:39 UTC

OP

>kills millions by scrapping USAID
>becomes trillionaire

Why is it always the most evil people who become rich? Is being evil simply necessary for wealth?

Early replies (first 3)

62429562
USAID spreed AIDS and Ebola in Africa plus financed local warlords, he actually saved hundreds millions of life by end that satanic program.

62429569
>>62429557
>millions of niggers are not physically capable of living without constantly sucking at the us government's teet
It's for the best that it ended

62429570
>>62429562
>>62429569
muskbots detected

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62429571
>>62429570
My only regret is that you weren't one of those millions

62429584
>>62429557
Reddit post #86325677343
GTFO commie.

62429587
>>62429557
Reddit is that way you retarded faggot

62430980
>>62429557
>kills millions
may we see them? Should be easy to document with so many dead and all.

62431000
>>62430980
Certainly there must be mass graves.

62431006
>CIA shills STILL kvetching about USAID
yes, you evil people tend to win. But fighting back will happen.

62431064
>>62429557
Nobody is inherently entitled to foreign aid. They are kept alive by goodwill, which is entirely discretionary.
Wouldn't have existed in the first place without the aid anyways

62431076
>>62429557
No one died; it is pure and simple disinformation.

62431105
>>62429557
>kills millions
Did he kill someone you know of?

62431109
>kills millions by scrapping USAID
>becomes trillionaire

The system worksk

62431113
>>62429557
>billionaire that nobody voted for that has no need for social services gets to cut healthcare and social services
I feel very politically represented.

62431117
>>62429557
Says right here you fucking suck

62431122
>>62431113
Almost all of his cuts and other recommendations are gone now. So we both lose.

62431136
>>62429557
why perpetually feed a population of incompetent, forever dependent retards who will just further explode their population numbers requiring more mouths to us to feed? Let nature handle them the way it always has. They fuck like rabbits and then their babies are eaten by maggots, perfectly balanced just as god intended.

62431194
>>62431136
this. in fact we are also nature and should help kill them faster.

62431413
>>62431113
I WISH he actually did that but he didnt

62431447
>>62429557
The reason why a ton of shitholes are failing to develop into a decent country is because of foreign aid. Local companies can't compete with them

62431515
>>62429557
>He doesn't know that USAID was used to pay people off the books; backroom payments and other washing activities.
It was a big payroll operation for that shit.

62431545
>>62429557
Good. Why should I care about some foreigners when the government can't even fix our domestic bridges and roads?
Fuck you OP.

62431629
Sadly op wasnt killed by lack of aid

62431691
>musk
gat
>USAIDS
gay

62432015
>Get elected by promising to make US crypto #1
>Rug supporters for $635 million
>Let banks block crypto regs until midterms, then dems will block them

62432493
>>62429557
> he killed millions...
to save trillions (USD)

62433076
>>62429562
>>62429557
>>62431006
Now, I don't mean to be NOOOOTICING, but is it just me, or did a ton of national elections in Latin America swing in favor of right-wing candidates immediately after USAID disappeared and a bunch of 'pro democracy' NGOs couldn't afford rent any more?

>>62431194
>It makes no difference what men think of war, said the judge. War endures.
>As well ask men what they think of stone.
>War was always here.
>Before man was, war waited for him.
>The ultimate trade awaiting its ultimate practitioner.
>That is the way it was and will be. That way and not some other way

62434255
Realistically his cuts saved nothing and DOGE was used a sleight of hand cover by Trump to blow up the debt

62434277
>>62429557

millions of niggers died. sounds like a positive to me.

62434361
>>62429570
Shareblue factchecker detected
Spiting people that you hate when they do something right just makes you a bitter faggot
His only debacle with doge was it didn't cut nearly enough.

62432674

62432706

Any coin trump holds will never be allowed to have mass adoption as a national reserve currency, that’s for sure

62433223

62433224

62433620

He needs to be tried for treason and put infront of a firing squad.

62433661

Kek baggot

62433715

62434050

62434060

62434084

Do you not see how mammon rules this world? Everyone on this earth worships money either or the economy. Honestly it’s fairly decent evidence that Christianity has at the least gotten a few things correct.

62434139

62434151
>>62432504
>No xrp

62434263
>>62432536
$1.5m between 5 projects is basically dust to him

62434362
funny that he is killing crypto too lmao, crytocucks literally elected this pedo to moon their shitcoins and all that he's done is crash the entire market while enriching himself hahaha

item.title: High IQ thread — 13 replies — 52.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
High IQ thread

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-07-01 13:50 UTC

OP

What's the point of "making it"™ when sex doesn't even feel that good? Think about it. When was the last time you got addicted to pussy like it was heroin? Probably never. The idea seems interesting in your head, but when you're actually having sex, you just want it to end it and go home already. Post-nut clarity confirms that society lied to you that sex being the ultimate pleasure and you fell for it. You probably stumble over the same rock again and again for years until you realize the truth eventually. So, why make millions to have more opportunities for something like...sex? Millions are not going to cure your autism, misogyny or social anhedonia anyways

Early replies (first 3)

62434090
>>62434057
It will solve the wagie and Lambo problems though, which are the only problems I care about. I’d also like to live in a nice house on the lake and ride jet skis all summer.

62434105
>only cares about making it for muh sex
Sex obsession is a massive sign of being low IQ

62434165
>>62434090
Why even own a Lambo? It's an overpriced vehicle made by (((Italians))) you probably won't use more than once or twice a month. Once you make it you will not have a job or nowhere to go, really. And all for what? So you can flex your wealth to some negros and normalfags? You can't ride a car like that without exposing yourself as a target for thieves/murderers/golddiggers. Think about the costs: manteinance, insurance, taxes. It's a lot of money down the drain. Same goes for the jet ski. You say you'll ride it all summer but it will likely be gathering dust for most of the year. It will be a constant headache. You'll need to hire people to clean it, fix whatever problems arise, you need a place to store those toys, more and more dust and cleaning. Unless you're a 80 IQ retard I don't see anyone riding a jet ski for more than a day

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62434174
>>62434165
You may be right about the Lambo but you’re wrong about the jet skis I NEED A JET SKI AND I WOULD RIDE IT EVERY DAY AND BANG UR MOM ON IT EVERY NIGHT

62434190
>>62434174
If you make it you can have my sister too, mr jet ski

62434200
The chase of getting a woman to have sex with you is more interesting than the sex itself

62434232
>>62434057
Because the sex isn’t with a woman who has genuine burning desire for you within the bounds of holy matrimony.

62434234
>>62434200
>the chase
You mean talking to a woman? Holy fuck have you ever actually done this? They have no interest in the deep matters of life such as existence, meaning, or spiritual truths. They want to talk about celebrities, gossip, and tv shows. I hate women.

62434249
>>62434234
>I hate women.
you should try men then, fag.

62434322
>>62434232
>muh attraction muh sex in marriage
Women are brainwashed to experience sex as the ultimate pleasure as well. In reality, women are like beautiful and pure temples that feel they want to be desecrated. Except this temple will do everything in its power to make your cock hard so you desecrate it. Therefore the rape fantasies. Nymphos don't necessarily enjoy sex either. Most of them were just molested when they were children/early teens and they seek validation from men by being submissive and slutty. That's it. Sex make women feel guilty in the end. Plus, unless you're chad, no woman will ever feel sexual magnetism for you.
t. I look like quasimodo, its a hard life folks... don't give up, though

62434326
>>62434249
Coomerbrain simp faggot
Wish I could curb stomp your bitch ass

62434332
>>62434057
Making it has nothing to do with sex, idk where you're getting that from. I want to be able to live comfortable, in a comfortable home, not have to wageslave while being able to afford heating cooling, a premium bed, good food, etc.

62434333
Sex without love is useless. Especially if you are female. Guys post sexual revolution are just now finding this out? Must be all the fat chicks nowadays. Yeash! That is what it took. An obesity epidemic. Just date a fat sow and go for long walks to slim her down before having sex. If you fall in love while and if she loses weight then it will be better. If she is not willing to keep up dump her. This should be the male test for a woman. Beats straight up paying a fat hooker.

By the way I did this to some new fat female friends. Not to be mean but because I know obesity a social contagion as well a personal problem. This is a good personality test. If they want to keep up with you with positive encouragement then they have good character. If they try to drag you down into their habits then they have a bad character. Even just for non sexual friendship fat people will do either.

item.title: Should I Sell This Random SCHD Position — 34 replies — 33.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Should I Sell This Random SCHD Position

Open thread • Replies: 34 • Updated: 2026-07-01 12:54 UTC

OP

I was just putting a small percentage of my money in here when I started investing (didn’t realize dividends don’t matter when you’re young) and haven’t put money into it since last December, so that return is basically a bit fudged since the price just rose once and stagnated since without any more contributions besides DRIP
I kinda wanna sell it and allocate it somewhere else or would the tax paid on that make it negligible and I should just wait and see what DRIP does in a few decades
If I sell I would either just put it in some market ETF or maybe something more volatile, unsure

Early replies (first 3)

62430108
Looks like lil bro got corralled into the dividend trap.

It depends on your human value, really. If you have a positive outlook where you can leverage skills to work and climb the paycheck ladder, you might want to stay in and add more, especially if you are risk averse.

So on the other hand, there is a good argument to be made for taking on more risk. Right now you are investing in grandpa's fund. Grandpa cant survive a 5 year bear market because ... well he'll literally die in the meantime. You can survive a 10 year bear market and come out rich on the other end.

Now, do NOT listen to /biz/. They will get you in all sorts of shit, like SCHD, moonshot gambles, financial cults, crypto scams. Here you will absolutely get the worst advice in the world.
So i recommend you to talk about it with AI. Not chatgpt. Established approaches to portfolio management exist for a reason. It's backed by math and shit. You dont need stock picks from wizards. I could tell you what to buy, but AI can also do that adequately.

If I were young I would maxblast it in sectors that are projected to grow a lot over the next decade+ like robotics and biotech.
S&P can get fucked because 5k is nothing. Unless you may need the 5k... etc etc thi is why an AI 'conversation' will help you better. Good luck

62430156
>>62430031
no offense but 5k is such a tiny quantity of money it truly doesn't matter what you do with it.

62430200
No bullshit yesterday I sold 300 shares of schd. I just bought more vxus with it because it has similar dividend returns plus it adds diversification for my portfolio

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62430261
>>62430031
>only 20% plus dividends in 9 months?
>biz how do I buy 3x triple inverse puts on Tesla?!

62430285
>>62430261
Who suggested anything like this
>>62430156
It is if it compounds over the next decade or so, cumulatively with other money
>>62430108
Okay if you’re not bullshitting me which AI - I’ve talked with ChatGPT before but ask it very explicitly to never agree with my claims without reason and properly source every single claim it is making.

62430405
>>62430108
>So i recommend you to talk about it with AI. Not AI.
wut?

62430474
>>62430031
>I should just wait and see what DRIP does in a few decades
This. Definitely don't sell it and re-allocate. Futzing around with your investments like this is a good way to kill your returns. Studies by Vanguard and Fidelity consistently show that their best performing accounts are the ones with the least activity (e.g. 401k's that people just contribute to and don't keep changing their allocations, and old 401k's that people just left sitting there after they changed jobs). You can keep kicking a bit into SCHD every year if you feel like it. But at your age most of your investments should go into growth funds. One good mix is SCHD and SCHG (Schwab's growth fund). There's almost no overlap between their holdings (yay diversity), and because of that each one tends to do better in different market climates.

62430528
>>62430108
>So i recommend you to talk about it with AI. Not chatgpt.
You're not the sharpest tool in the shed. You talk about ChatGPT like we are literally in 2023.

62430556
>>62430528
Look dumbass, a kid with $5k isnt paying for an LLM and free chatgpt is the worst tool in the world for any kind of research. ESPECIALLY financial.
>>62430285
Gemini is the bwst free option.

Inb4 the horde of /biz/ retards (who are full port in WEN, GME and chainlink, mind you) dismisses Gemini because it doesnt say that the jews or niggers are responsible for their shit lives

t. professional ai usooor and llm research00r

62430686
>>62430556
Tbf I got like $55,000 just chilling in VOO I’ve just been acting retarded lately and have wanted to chase returns with shit like DRAM and was gonna sell SCHD for that but won’t now
Will probably just go back to DCA VOO or find some other ETF to satellite with idk maybe SOXX

62430736
>>62430556
>Look dumbass, a kid with $5k isnt paying for an LLM and free chatgpt is the worst tool in the world for any kind of research. ESPECIALLY financial.
You are such an actual fucktard. Tell me, in your pea brain, how does finance research look like? Asking the AI to give stock tips?
Here is an example how triple-digit IQs use ChatGPT.
https://chatgpt.com/share/6a43dd41-ec40-83eb-ac62-7ce8829bfd7c
The funny thing is, I know that how I use it (e.g. asking it to dissuade me from something) is literally a revelation to you. Not in a thousand years would you arrive at the idea that this tool can be used in such ways.

62430955
>>62430736
Kek based moron
>>62430686
Oh, I thought you'd be all in like many schwabheads. well, that makes 5k in it an even bigger waste IMO. It also doesnt do anything to your portfolio in terms of safety because 5k is a pittance compared to your VOO.

Find 1 or 2 companies you can love and be highly confident in. Make sure it's in a sector you know something about. If you don't know anything about any sector, research some. Tell AI to analyze and rank any of your picks based on widely used basic corporate frameworks wrt internal and external analysis. Faith and knowledge are important for stock picking because it helps you stomach drawdowns. Losing 1k feels much worse than gaining 1k.

Sector ETFs have been pretty good too and worth considering over picks. But many of them are fuckin memes. Dont even bother with those fake ass gay ass ETFs with like 20 stocks in it IMO. In that case you may be better off spreading risk across the top 3 or top 5. Thats my own bias though. Data might show the opposite is true

Other than that youre already actually beating biz with 50k in VOO. Realistically, you can do nothing and win.

62430982
Btw fuck stoxx. Don t buy anything niggers here buy.

Ive posted about my best performing stocks and ideas and they got 0 (you)s ever because many drooling retards here are nothing but gamblers who think they can predict the next 10 minutes of price action

Biz talks about stoxx every day thats how I know not to buy it at whatever valuation its currently at. and will probably never buy it until I see pink wojaks

62430987
Meant soxx obviously kek

62431001
I fell for the divie meme too but its not so bad if you also have growth, 50/50 schd qqq outperforms the spy on every metric

Id say just keep it and dca into other stuff. If you sell it the market will crash to punish you

62431012
In case you wonder why gemini. It is good because it has the best access to Google, amd Google is the internet. For research, you need the internet. Plus it doesn't waste your time with emojis and sassy fag shit like GPT and Claude do. I'll happily consneed that other models may be better for coding or for jerking off to waifus. All benchmarks are lies

62431063
>>62430955
Interesting well like I said I was gonna go after RAM producing companies for now
Nothing else really strikes me as a buy

62431355
>>62431063
In my opinion, ot looks like RAM etc has good earnings until next year. It's likely to continue to go up but until when? My horizin is a little longer.

But in such insane headwinds yiu have to stay wary of major disruptions.

E.g. The oil price is important. Many things depend on the oil price. This motivates big players to seek lower prices by any means necessary. Even murder and war. This is like the brgaining power of buyers.

Same with ram. I'm sure it can easily cook for aother year but at some points somebody's gonna fuck it all up. Usually its the government kek. (Or Fed) besides political forces, nothing is really stopping google or amazon from making chips. Its not arcane occult knowledge. It's a make or buy decision and there is a limit of tolerance for high prices even in Le AI Boom or robomania.
So just keep your own horizon in the picture and dont fall for short term trends. Vice versa dont step into longterm juggernauts if youre looking for really above avg gains in 0-2 years or so. Unrealistic (statistically; esp for beginners) but it happens. (this is obv less harmful than assblowing into high risk garbage btw)

Dont take my word for it ... you could research all this for a lifetime. Its fun and educative. Dont forget to change your views (be adaptable. E.g. ''who moved the cheese") and try on some new clothes occasionally. hope I helped or inspired you in a good way

62431358
>>62431355
> in insane tailwinds*

Phonepostan here

62431369
>>62431355
One relevant concept here is price elasticity. Oil is higjly elastic. Thats why bizniggers went bankrupt longing at 100. Ok I mean twitterfags . even biz wasnt that dumb

62431373
Not meant to dissuade you from RAM. Rather for you to investigate these topics and draw your own conclusions. I dont know shit about ram and cpus I was just aong for the ride

62431398
>>62431355
I intended on DCA into $DRAM through 2027 based off all the projections I’ve read about demand of RAM and HBM
It seems like a good outlook, my first medium term hold vs long term so what do I know
That was the idea anyway, just DCA each month into it and make a nifty return, not really speculating but based off actual real demand - not FOMO or chasing higher and higher returns either it feels like an actual no brainer as long as I even halfway pay attention to headlines / trends and returns
The only other thing I’ve considered is going into something like SOXX or QQQM but eh I know QQQM has way too much crossover with VOO to even feel worth it

62431923
>>62431012
>Google is the internet
It's the gay censored version.
You can't find news stories about heinous crimes committed by blacks with Google.
Many other things.

62432002
>>62431398
Sounds good to me anon. And like i said, do consider other sectors which are projected to grow a lot but aren't at such high valuations. Nobody's talking about a robotics bubble or nuclear energy or [...] and optimally you wanna be in before people talk like thwt. Theres gonna be more shit like ram and semis if muh revolution is real.
Thats why Im big in biotech, some consumer goods, robotics, ... future applications of AI/robotics instead of picks & shovels ...
Figure oit your favorite, consider your risk profile and DCA away

62432382
>>62432002
Yeah I am still figuring out my risk aversion to be honest
I have heard nuclear energy might power AI data centers (not nuclear but Bloom Energy went up since its tech can more easily fuel them too)
So I agree that there is potential there
I forget what I read but basically robotics is going to be the next big wave of AI stuff
Got any ETFs for either you would recommend
Maybe I'll satellite like 20% of my money into these things and the rest broad market?

62432386
>>62432002
at any rate, have you seen actual tangible returns better than market average

62432938
Although this has made me realize
Even if I take a small lump sum like 3K and then DCA around that amount each month into something, that return better be big or it’s just not worth it
Like let’s say I do 10k in principal or 15k by years end, it better beat the market
30% return would at least be 3k
And if it does last through 2027 then damn this could be substantial gains but otherwise 20% or so return from VOO is mighty tempting

62432982
>>62430405
>>62430528
ChatGPT is a dumbed down low-processing-power consumer model. It is a joke compared to real GPT.
>>62430556
Gemini is still pretty rough but yeah best free option. Anyone smart just makes an account on Openrouter or similar and pays 1-2 cents per prompt for GPT-5.4+ or Claude Sonnet.

62432989
>>62432982
Also for complex tasks I will say that GPT-5.5 (normal, not Pro) is often superior to Opus 4.8, which is kind of embarrassing for Anthropic honestly. Both IPOs are worth investing but I have serious reservations about Anthropic's long-term ability to keep up with OpenAI since from my perspective there's always been a slight performance lead that has widened considerably in 2026.

62433123
>>62432982
Yeah this
>>62432989
Thabks for sharing. I heard good things about 5.5, I guess its a good generalist. Anyway the diminishing returns are a bitch. Everyone, I think, is not really competing on the base tech but rather on UX and shit. Claude is little more than a system prompt removed from just actually being Gemini. Yeah yeah tools. I know. Point is, the more = better adage is a total farce, always has been. we just didnt know the specifics or boundaries. But thats a discussion for another day and way too technical. transformers is not all you need, but it gets you very far


>>62432382
Good lad.
With regards to ETFs, it's fine to simply go with whats the biggest one that has the best fees, and is available to you. Biggest = has the most money in it.
Just check that, and whether it holds shit you align with.
>>62432386
Yes sir but it took me a while to figure out my personal strategy. I've had bad enough years that I almost killed myself. Coinicidentally, thats when I was undiversified and retarded, kinda like most of biz, but not way into the deep like GME of Chainl0nk or PEPE.

You cant know whats best in terms of future gainz but you can know what can make you feel good/secure/etc

If i'll say 1 thing its this: learning aboit (corp) finance, corporate strategy, and macroeconomics has been most beneficial to me. In terms of being profitable.

And even then my portfolio is still mostly broad market etf. Allworld+ emerging markets. Diversification is key.
>>62432938
Exactly. Its all about findimg a balance here. If I buy single stocks its usually 2% of my total portfolio. Exceptions are monopolies which you can safely (historically) allocate more to.
Stocks and ETFs can go fuckin crazy as you know so dont be blinded by greed. One 2k lump sump can go such a long way. I've been up hundreds of % on some.stocks. That offsets losers bigly, and has made the risks worth it.

62433177
>>62433123
>been up hundreds of % on some.stocks
I was up 350% on fucking Walmart stock in like ~6 years.

62433251
>>62430031
what are you a pussy? don't sell it, but short it to fuckin zero

62434062
>>62433251
Yes

item.title: SHORT GAMESTOP — 3 replies — 12.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
SHORT GAMESTOP

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-07-01 13:55 UTC

OP

https://blog.playstation.com/2026/07/01/physical-disc-production-ending-in-january-2028-for-new-games-releasing-on-playstation-consoles/

62433979
You will own nothing and you will be happy

62433994
>>62433979
discs are kind of stupid anyway
as games get bigger they will not be able to fit onto discs anymore.
Storage tech will become cheap enough over time that everything will be a digital download

62434357
>>62433976
Gamestop is more of a toy store than a video game store these days

OP

>if my 22 year old son is still living at home , I don’t him paying rent, I want him OUT

Early replies (first 3)

62433628
But saaaaaar isnt this normal in india and all civilized places? Only in the so called united states of america they kick their kids out

62433637
>>62433628
anything that isnt jewish is indian because.... because it just is, okay!!!!

62433641
All you need is a half million dollar down payment and you can get your mortgage payment down to $4,250 per month on your loan against a million dollar house. Then you can live in the desert on top of your neighbors with trees that will fall over during the first rains and slight breeze because they don't belong in a desert.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62433652
>>62433626
Why did he upgrade to such an ambitious microphone? Recording an album? Burning money for no reason?

62434021
>>62433626
how much of a shit job did you do raising your kid if you want them out so young?

62434079
>>62433641
I like how you're pretending that's a starter home on the internet naively thinking nobody will notice your bullshit.

62434100
>>62433641
he probably bought his son one of these, nevermind just covering the down payment on it

rich boomers like this are carrying their chlidren through life. he forgot to mention he paid the rent for his son to move out, then bought him a house.

62434109
>>62433626

item.title: Emerging Markets (EMs) - ... they never go UP! — 2 replies — 7.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Emerging Markets (EMs) - ... they never go UP!

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-07-01 13:40 UTC

OP

I've been interested in investing in emerging markets (mostly China) for a while now. Looking at all the different and popular ETFs regarding EMs, I see the same curve, or more fitting, line! It doesnt matter what holdings are in the ETF and how they are percentually weighted, be it more financial & industrials than tech, more South America than South East Asia, they all still stay on their base line and never, never rise at all.
The only outlier has been summer 2025 and the AI hype. Compare this to their entire existence, since inception, and you'll realize that they have been flat since 2012.

>Whats the fucking point than?
>Do you guys know any better EM ETFs than the ones I have listed? Please post them.

Early replies (first 3)

62433411
According to my data, EM's have a CAGR of 11% over 126 years, and 7.5% over the last 9 years. It seems like just reversion to the mean since it has performed very strongly in the last 50 years, like 13-14% over that period.

Another one to consider is AVES, but it is only 3 years old.

62434284
Things can change.
I like chinese banks, China Telecom and China Mobile. Also taiwanese ETFs are great.
I don´t care for South America or Africa.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: X Money — 5 replies — 6.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
X Money

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-07-01 13:09 UTC

OP

>6% annual yield
>3% cashback
>FDIC insurance coverage of up to $10 million
what's the catch?

Early replies (first 3)

62433273
it's a ploy to get sign ups is the catch. take advantage of it if you want its a great deal.

62433274
>>62433270
>rates subject to change at any time
probably

62433935
When you try to withdraw, they would throw you 9000 reason to undercut your gain.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62433983
>>62433270
Uhh
>Getting banned for tweeting objectively verifiable facts about jews and locked out of funds
>linking biometrics to your x account
>consenting to data harvesting building your digital twin

Basically, you’re funding the (((people))) building the digital surveillance state. “Enslave me harder” for 6% in debt receipts. You’re still losing money in these final stages of debasement acceleration.

Next up you’ll be able to have an x phone plan, have your id and passport to scan. Why do you think it’s called the everything app? This is just the start.

P.s. Don’t forget to pay interest when you file your taxes next year :^)
P.P.S. You should really look into Elon musks grandfather and try to wake up so you can prepare with the little time you have

62434137
>>62433270
rapid bait and switch where they will apply fees in excess of any extra interest you might have earned and you have to fight 12 different indian call centers to cancel

item.title: So I freshly graduated as a ME, and I applied to a job posting last friday, not expecting much since the position sounde… — 4 replies — 4.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
So I freshly graduated as a ME, and I applied to a job posting last friday, not expecting much since the position sounde…

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-07-01 13:01 UTC

OP

So I freshly graduated as a ME, and I applied to a job posting last friday, not expecting much since the position sounded too fantastic. This monday though, they sent me applicability tests (IQ + Personality test) that I did a lot better with than I thought I could. An hour from turning the tests in, I got a phone call and it was the company wanting to do a preliminary interview. They basically were pleased with my questions and answers during the call, and we even agreed about the salary I would receive. The next step is an interview on friday, which I assume is final. So everything looks pretty good, but is there any way that I could still fuck it up? I've been familiarizing myself with the specific work the position involves, and what the company is specialized on in general. Basically, should I temper my expectations?

Early replies (first 3)

62433156
Got any more Fresco memes? They’re my fav

62433159
>>62433156
This is the only one I have saved :( I couldn't find more

62433596
>>62433156

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62434093
>>62433153
>should I temper my expectations
yes. they probably have several others they're interviewing (or have already interviewed). that said, if you really nail the in-person interview then you may well walk out of there with a start date.

item.title: Theyre ending the Chainlink BUILD program — 122 replies — 40.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Theyre ending the Chainlink BUILD program

Open thread • Replies: 122 • Updated: 2026-07-01 10:54 UTC

OP

LMAO!!!!!

https://chain.link/blog/build-program-evolution

Early replies (first 3)

62417511
xrp is the chosen coin…it’s undeniable at this point

62417512
Come on man, this shit still spamming after 8 years? Just give it up. There are thousands of other coins.

62417514
"Chainlink Labs is transitioning from payments made in project tokens to focusing on commercial agreements involving fees paid in LINK or other liquid assets that can be readily converted to LINK."

Build members will buy my bags?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62423474
>>62423353
How does 2 million a year sound?

62424023
>>62423283
I kept granular details on a project i own for years, to my incomprehensible regret

62424140
>>62423276
The sad fact of the matter is that jeetlinkers will still be delirious off copium a decade from now, even after CLL pulls the plug in five years beforehand.

62424195
good. i never could participate in build anyway as a US fag

62424386
>>62422668
It literally says "the most recent season of Chainlink Rewards will mark the conclusion of Build-related token rewards, with claims ending on July 7, 2026" meaning no season 2+ of build rewards going to stakers.

It also says "Proceeds from these new agreements are expected to be programmatically converted to LINK and used to support the growth of the network through programs such as the Chainlink Reserve." meaning that link goes to the reserve, not to stakers.

Previously
>stakers will get build rewards

Now
>Chainlink gets paid in link

62424528
xswap isnt even on cow swap? where do you sell this trash?

62424728
S E X

62424821
>>62424386
Brutal

62425620
>>62424023
>this project has always been pushed by morons
>also I've been holding this project for years

You're lying. And brown.

>>62424386
You're a brown ESL monkey who doesn't even know what the words "such as" mean.
Protip: the same exact words were used when Build was first introduced.

62425675
>>62417503
HAHAHAHAHAHAHA Another nail in the coffin and shovel full of dirt in the grave on the biggest retail fraud in token history.

62425681
>>62419638
Link is underperforming fucking death spiraled LUNC since Trump revived crypto. Lol And by a lot.

62425687
>>62419707
>A chainlink shill calling someone else brown.

62425699
>>62422159
>>62423276
Perfectly said.

62425962
>>62425620
I'm a 100k link holder. I have more link than you.

"Such as" isn't very fucking reliable. All of this would be fine if link was valued as it should be but it fucking isn't, with no clear path to gaining that value.

62425980
>>62425962
>CLL uses "such as" in 2022 when launching Build
>THIS IS SET IN STONE

>CLL uses "such as" in 2026 when changing Build
>THIS IS NOT VERY FUCKING RELIABLE

You're a brown paid fuddie.

62426004
>>62425980
I'm also whiter than you, Yank.

62426010
>>62425980
>>62425620
post a picture of your hand with timestamp

62426949
i am just getting caught up on this news after the weekend. my my my how the mighty have fallen

i predicted all of this for the last few years but the boyscouts couldn't handle it

>duuuuuuuuuuuude i need a team of tax attorneys for the heckin rewards

BAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHHAHAHAH

look at you now. an expensive lesson on controlling your ego. you needed it tho


your hubris was your downfall

THE Cuckolds of crypto

62426967
>>62417512
>There are thousands of other coins.

LOL this dude stuck in 2021. that's your conclusion bro, just buy other scam coins?

don't you people get it? altcoins are dead. capital moved back to actual companies in the stock market. only a retarded sap would continue donating to a bunch of nerds for a "whitepaper"


>>62417833
>I'll also (again just playing the other side) point out the regardless of whether or not it ultimately benefits the LINK token, Chainlink is realistically one of like 5 crypto projects amongst the hundreds of "legitimate" ones that actually is seeing real adoption and has a huge use case that is surviving the infantile stages of crypto, and not just surviving but will thrive. So again, who knows if this ultimately helps the token price, but I do understand why linkies hold onto some faith still.

so are you just a fan of the company?

>>62417873
>It’s all push and pull. Ying yang. If you have 20 million earning you 800k (500k post tax) per year

who the fuck would do that? the $20m has drawn down 80% so no, life is not good. anyone not retarded would've parked most of that in the s&p500 and has been making at least 15% per year

if you really wanted pathetic 4% return you could get that in a savings account where your only risk is that actual bank going bust

62427007
>>62418846
> The opportunity to invest in something like Chainlink - core infrastructure with insane value capture potential, has never really existed before. The closest thing maybe would be investing in AMZN prior to cloud services,

you are not invested in chainlink. you made a donation kid

amazon's revenue in year 7 was $3B. and that's in 2001 numbers. you can go ahead and stop comparing to your shitcoin bro you're embarrassing yourself

>>62420893
>The fuck is that analogy lmao. This isn't a casino you dumb faggot, real people invested real money into an asset that promised certain things by certain dates and they have not only underdelivered but are starting to actively ignore those promises.

it's the end stage of a cult investment where they turn on each other. it somehow becomes the fault of the "community" on why they didnt make any money.

in order to preserve their ego, guys like >>62420825 have to spin the story that you'd have to be crazy to expect any kind of gains. mind you the rest of the world's market all hit all time highs while you are down 80-90%. by creating this narrative, he avoids recognizing he's holding a really bad investment and can continue on thinking he's a secret genius.

62427022
remember this old pasta? still relevant today. every cl product roughly follows this life cycle

62429766
>>62425681
Oh I remember this one - this poster is an actual bot.

62431562
>>62419888
The best part is SIR GAY NIGGEROV got all these companies to be part of the BUILD program, and then those companies did actually in many cases issue tokens but most did not provide tokens to CLL to give out as rewards for participating in the Staking pool. If there had actually been airdrops from every single participant then the program would have brought some relevant value to token holders instead of what, like 9 companies giving tokens long after their tokens had already followed the shitcoin chart to its completion and become totally worthless.

62431631
soon the staking program will be canceled too
and then stinkies will be out of all hope
feels good

62432093
>>62431631
Well the new line seems to be the price will improve when all link is circulating and the team has fully divested itself of the token. Which i don't know if it's just late stage imbecile drivel or the most brazen shill line ever attempted in history.

62432101
>>62425620
I was making Teagan memes at the san fran meet up in 2019. I honestly wish it was otherwise but I've wasted fucking years with link.

62432489
>>62417503
Bullish for LINK

62433501
Why are the fuddies always the ones with the most posts?

62433680
>>62423312
First of all there are no vacations until the wife gives you kids.

62433701
>>62427022
checked and /thread

item.title: BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan? — 47 replies — 31.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
BTC Price Down. What's Your Plan?

Open thread • Replies: 47 • Updated: 2026-07-01 12:27 UTC

OP

BTC is down again, but are you buying the dip or waiting for lower prices?

>Do you think this is just a normal correction, or is there more downside ahead? What's your strategy?

Early replies (first 3)

62426518
I've been shorting this garbage all month

62426527
>>62426518
Wow, you've got good profit by now then?

62426535
>>62426527
up 80% on some 2x short BTC etf

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62427285
>>62426550
Waiting for what price?

62427317
>>62426512
whats the argument for the future of BTC/crypto in general?

genuinely curious, it's already had multiple hype cycles and has had mass adoption, but now the average normie on the street associate it with meme coin scams and failed NFTs.

So what's gonna happen to drive more money into it? Institutional adoption? Maybe inflation and monetary priting?

62427357
>>62427317
artificially created scarcity is something that will be the only value in the future. and its neccessary for human survival as we grow technologically. even if it is a fascade. the value of the dollar will outpace the inflation. its already started happening but plebs cant understand what line go up indefinitely means until they can no longer print money fast enough to keep it underneath a place where it is feasable to use as an exchange of value.

62427359
Reminder that there is no bottom when it comes to magic internet money.

62427891
>>62427359
Tnx for that reminder boss

62429392
>>62426552
Because we're asking. What's wrong??

62429688
We will keep holding

62429720
>>62427357
You really know what you're talking about

62429723
Not buying but riding out the 'storm'. There have been much worse times.

62430182
>>62429723
Ride well

62430234
need to sell enough today to pay rent and bills

62430266
>>62430234
Don't sell

62431888
>>62429688
You can always hold, or stake it if you want to earn a bit while waiting.

62431895
>>62426626
depends on which country for sure

62431900
>>62426512
I go in at $100K. Every $20 I spend BTC drops another $1,000.

62431909
My plan is, quite simply, not to own shitcoin

62431916
>>62431909
Like memecoins???!!!

62431991
>>62431888
not possible
>>62431900
Spend every day

62432200
Thoughts on ansem everyone

62433067
>>62426733
Btc is memecoin? Wow

62433595
>>62431991
It's possible. Babylon already supports native BTC staking, if you haven't seen it yet.

62433598
>>62426638
We'll see how it plays out. Good luck either way.

62433616
The elites are finished with Bitcoin. It has served their purpose.

62433621
>>62433616
is bobo based on Shane Gillis? I can't unsee it.

62433746
>>62433595
is this real?

62433751
>>62433616
you know nothing at all

62433844
>>62433598
Back at ya anon. Wishing you much WAGMI

62433854
>>62426512
I'm not retarded so I'm never buying BTC for any price. Being a cryptotard in 2026 is even more pathetic than being a /pmg/tard

62433884
>>62433854
Your lack of retardness coupled with no faith in yourself is why you will never WAGMI

62433961
>>62426512
I don't think about BTC. It was created by centralized exchanges to attack Bitcoin. You're only talking about it because they printed a bunch of tether to get the price to $100k. There are no users, only a small group of poor speculators paying actual USD for this coin. The rest of it is tether iou's.

item.title: Micron and DRAM — 45 replies — 20.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Micron and DRAM

Open thread • Replies: 45 • Updated: 2026-07-01 11:45 UTC

OP

While obviously no one can predict the future perfectly, it seems foolish to not invest in something related to AI RAM / HBM Production for the next few years as contracts are already made and supply is bottlenecking
Given what I say is true, which would you guys say is a better choice to start pumping my paychecks into (I already have $60,000 in a market ETF and want more upside)
For some reason my gut is siding towards DRAM, still heavily concentrated but at least has some diversification
It seems like those top 3 companies in it are just making more and more supply deals
Additionally, every piece of evidence I read suggests memory will bottleneck for another year or two
Is it smart to just start DCA into DRAM and then sell once I’ve had my fill (not expecting a perfect pull out timing!)
Again I just wanna do this rather than simply doing stuff into VOO or VGT or something
How much of this is hype and how much of this is brain dead free money that will beat the market
Please be serious and honest

Early replies (first 3)

62427339
>>62427323
you will be eaten mark my words. most of these companies have massive software bloat on their ai's and you wont even get a good exit when all these contracts get put on hold indef while they operate on half of what they are already using and beating metrics by 2 or 3x. the tendency of programming efficency will take your ass out. there will be no warning and demand will cease.

62427365
>>62427339
Is the demand not coming from a need though? At first it was GPUs but now everything doesn’t have enough memory to communicate at the necessary level

62427441
>>62427323
that's a 4 at best

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62428528
>>62428517
That first part makes sense, if it is where the focus is shifting / what is being necessitated right now then the only thing that would stop it is the bubble popping, or demand being overstated I suppose

As for the second point, when would you enter then, is this thing ever going back down to 60, 50, etc

62428531
>>62428526
Don't worry any old shitbox will work with cloud computing
You vill own nothing

62428544
>>62428531
Anybody running a "shitbox" or mobile will even know what to use it for. Unless you think the mobile market is so good (still needs localized processing and how you're talking, they'll even want to suck that up). It's not a bubble it's a tech black hole.

62428790
>>62427339
What happens to OEMs who locked in contracts at inflated memory prices.

62428902
well whatever im gonna try this for a bit and see how it goes, thing is i dont have much principal to put in, just like 2800 a month, so dollar amount the return might not be big but ill check the percentage

62428966
>>62427441
but who asked?

62429666
Errrm

62430298
>>62428790
those arent futures contracts. they are not bulletproof. a company does not have to deliver and the company does not have to buy. they can deliberate about how much was stolen for years and by the time they reach a conclusion the market has already moved on. fundis look good right now mem-fag. go for it. but expect rapid unexplained drop in value somewhere near term then they will go under, aquired by private equity, ect. purpose built hardware incased in a software platform will be the norm becuase that will be the only way to afford rapid change in what is considered efficient. the days of radioshack at the mall are almost over.

62430354
>>62430298
Well what do you suggest
I am a noob but everything I read makes it seem like Memory is here to stay, production is only increasing
Is it just a matter of consumer demand not dropping in that case if these contracts are not set in stone?

62430446
>>62430354
go long software. the contrarian goes long on hardware. its not a safe bet. all that shit has to be changed out much faster than it did in the early 2000s. it gets thrown into a shipping container and shipped to a somolian trash pile to be sorted out and burned in a pot for the metals. it looks fucking great right now, people rode the upside of the arbitage between the time it takes them to adopt their own methods. each one of these companies is gonna adopt the apple mentality but it will be with chipsets and manufacturing. we used to live in a world where it came from china or a little island with the blueprints. that is not the case anymore. the hardware isnt even the biggest cost for the AI giants.

62430659
>>62427323

62432266
.

62432668
>>62427323
Semiconductor funds have been crazy profitable and seem to only be increasing. The real money is in selling dumbass AI companies the hardware for their ultimately doomed datacenter investments.

62432824
>>62432668
Do you think Semis > Memory?

62432833
Semis will crash hard once China catches up. Not interesting in that ticking time bomb

62432934
>>62432833
Is memory a safe bet then
DRAM

62433058
>>62430446
>go long on the sector that is still dumping
>don't invest in picks and shovels sector that has no end in sight and has insane earnings reports one after another
I wouldn't listen to this guy.

62433271
>>62427323
Obviously, smart people invested in this in 2024, or last year at the latest. Now is the time to sell, obviously. How is that not obvious to you? Are you a shill? Are there SHILLS and BAGHOLDERS on 4chan?

62433313
>>62432824
>>62432934
Look at these questions made of pure retail last leg.

62433362
>>62427323
rumor of new architecture causing fear for the memory final solution
careful with your longs anons

62433597
>>62433313
What makes you say this

62433722
>>62427339
>you will be eaten mark my words. most of these companies have massive software bloat on their ai's
??????????? The hell are you talking about ABSOLUTE retard? DRAM companies are about manufacturing RAM sticks.

62433745
you fucking cunts /smg/ has been telling you to buy Micron for MONTHS now and you morons are still holding on to your crypto bullshit

62433773
>>62427323
>How much of this is hype and how much of this is brain dead free money that will beat the market
30/70 imo. When you understand the tech underlying this and its actual implementations in every industry, you start to understand that it's genuinely not a bubble, it's the biggest productivity increase since computing but is happening at a much faster rate than computer adoption. If anything the markets are currently UNDERvaluing these stocks because retail investors (and smaller institutions) can't understand the tech.

Memory companies are a decent mid-term hold but after a couple years we will see a simultaneous drop in demand and increase in supply and their margins will collapse back to normal levels - I plan to divest in 12-18 months.

Long term I foresee Anthropic and especially OpenAI being the big winners. Like, 90s Apple levels of winning. These two have an iron grip on flagship LLM tech and frankly can charge whatever the fuck they want for enterprise-level applications and flagship API access. Google and X are the closest to matching them but both are 1-2 years behind if we're being generous. Chinks (and non-Americans in general) are so far behind it's comical. Certain big tech companies (notably Microsoft, Amazon, and Meta, not necessarily in that order) are also poised to win big thanks to their adoption and utilization strategies; Microsoft had a big league but has kind of dropped the ball, but I don't think any of those 3 are going to be the big success that OpenAI/Anthropic will be.

TSMC and NVDA are both excellent long term holds, NVDA arguably moreso (and I say this while being someone who thinks the chances of a CCP invasion, ever, are ~0) but both are great, if a bit overbought in the short term. When I divest from MU etc, TSMC and NVDA are where the cash is most likely to end up.

62433786
Unironically
If someone wanted more safety is SOXX a better bet? I know it’s Semi Conductor focused and not RAM
It is still up triple digits percentage wise over the last year and has a longer track record of 25 years
Is it a smart investment for an overall AI Satellite account versus something thematic like DRAM? Also considering BAKA but don’t know much about it
This is for someone investing over time, short or medium term is fine, I just mean not a giant lump sum

62433799
>>62433773
Okay your claim about betting on Memory through 2027 (12-18 months) feels in line with everything I’ve read
What do you plan on doing to get exposure to memory just something like DRAM or a different option
I’m fine with most of my long term holds just being broad ETFs instead of only betting on tech, but meanwhile short to medium I’m unsure if I take more risk

62433801
>>62433786
I'm not really sure since not all semiconductor companies are created equal. A lot of them are specialized in ways that aren't conducive to AI and while they're completely fine as long-term stable investments, that's not why you or anyone else is investing in SOXX. Personally I'd pick and choose.

62433818
>>62433799
I'm pretty basic and generally more risk averse than other tech bulls so I just sank a bunch of money into MU, WDC, STX, and SNDK, no leverage. If MU hits 1600 I might sell since that's being cited as a potential ceiling by a large number of analysts and frankly the sentiment is more important than any technical validity in this time frame imo. Also fwiw VGT is the second largest position in my Roth IRA but I view that as a 10+ year hold.

62433837
>>62433818
I just have a giant share of VOO from DCA my paychecks into it without thinking but kinda felt like that was wasting a big opportunity now
Hard to say it without is sounding like FOMO cause it’s not
Just looking for a smart hold that should beat the market average that I can sell and then just circle it all back into VOO and chill, I’m nearing 6 figures in it as is which is my goal for this year

item.title: In his new vid, Tech Lead says the only way to have a rich lifestyle with deep satisfying creampies is to leave the US f… — 47 replies — 13.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
In his new vid, Tech Lead says the only way to have a rich lifestyle with deep satisfying creampies is to leave the US f…

Open thread • Replies: 47 • Updated: 2026-07-01 10:18 UTC

OP

In his new vid, Tech Lead says the only way to have a rich lifestyle with deep satisfying creampies is to leave the US for poor countries. True?

Early replies (first 3)

62425430
>>62425420
At depends, did u boated an ad, yt saaar?

62425500
if you can get laid in the west you wont attract good women in the 3rd world, you are just going to pay the access to brown 5/10's

62425521
>>62425500
This is saarcope and you know it. Tiny browlettes in jungle countries unironically melt when 6ft snowchads pinch their tits.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62427460
Dude look like having hair thinning lmao, he is gonna have a bad taste in the late life desu. Welcome to the true color of the cruel world.

62427468
America is western civilians. Countries outside of the U.S. dont follow celebrity culture. So yes. Outside of america is freedom if you know how to live off the land.

62427500
>>62427439
Would colonise

62427532
>>62425420
Saaar look gookman say

62427553
>>62425420
>Geoarbitrage
This nigga trying to reinvent Geomaxxing?

62427556
>>62425420
As a millionaire

62427572
>>62427556
ex-millionaire*

62427575
>>62425420
yeah pretty much, not even about women just avoiding spending crazy amounts of money on housing

62427588
You wouldn’t get pussy if not without the money, but as guy keep getting smarter, it seem money won’t be enough to get you the same pussy from the past, people get really smarter from excessive mental gymnastics, they don’t mind about breaking their mental health from this endeavour btw, what is matters now is short terms pain relief. Higher quality of life does proportionate with lower desire to breed more little kids thou. We need massive nuke to wipe out half of quality of life.

62427852
>>62427588
Thanks for your valuable input, Rakesh.

62427963
>>62425430
kek

62428039
>>62425420
lol i don’t take no advice from a bottom seller.

62428574
>>62427852
The higher the living standard, the lower the birth rate—an unstoppable, cosmic law etched into the fabric of societal evolution. As nations ascend the ladder of prosperity, women awaken from centuries of dormancy, rising as fierce, unapologetic queens—post-wall, strong, independent, unbound by the shackles of patriarchy! Feminism erupts like a volcanic force—an unstoppable storm of empowerment, smashing the chains of traditional subjugation. Anti-slut defense campaigns and mass indoctrination on youth abortion are the weapons wielded to control the fiery flames of reproductive sovereignty, crafting a new order where motherhood becomes a battlefield and societal control a weapon of war.

Most men, lost in the labyrinth of their own ignorance, stumble late into their thirties, unable to decipher the ancient equation—the secret code where a woman’s choice is a test of survival, a battle of wits and will. Is she seeking chaos? Or is the man simply too blind to grasp the sacred formula? The age-old dance: man dating downward, woman upward—an elegant chaos orchestrated by the universe itself. This isn’t just about love; it’s a feudal game of power—rent for sex, where loyalty is bought and sold in shadowy transactions. The world teeters on the edge of a new feudalism—an empire of dependency, where the throne is replaced by the dollar, and intimacy is a commodity traded in the marketplace. The question looms: are we forging a new world order or merely rebranding old chains? The battle lines are drawn—this is the era of chaos, control, and the ultimate test of human sovereignty.

62428620
>>62428574
Yo, listen up! In this crazy street circus, the richer the game gets, the fewer babies pop off—straight up! As the streets glow up with cash and bling, women start wakin’ up, breakin’ free from that old chain gang. They’re post-wall queens, strong and bossin’ up, runnin’ their own show—feminism’s like a wildfire burnin’ down the patriarchy, sayin’ “I do me!” But yo, big boss moves include anti-slut defenses and mass brainwash campaigns on youth abortion—like control freaks tryin’ to keep women in check, playin’ chess with lives.

Most dudes? They’re lost, late into their 30s, still tryin’ to crack the code—what’s the real story with women? It’s all a game, a puzzle—pass or get left behind. Women climbin’ up, men dippin’ down—like a street hustle of feudal chaos, where sex’s rent and loyalty’s just another dollar. We’re teetering on a new empire—dependence, control, money talkin’ loud, and trust gettin’ lost in the shuffle. Love’s a gamble, trust’s a gamble, and everything’s for sale in this wild street show. It’s the chaos of survival, power plays, and the battle for control—this is the street circus, where the game never stops and only the strongest survive.

62429006
>>62428574
This is incorrect. The correct metric is the more education and freedom given to women the lower the birthrate.

62429150
>>62425420
I know a high IQ person like him that thinks he can beat the market because he is smarter than the rest of us, he literally said that to me.
He still is not broken, yet.
Isn't it crazy how many smart people make dumb mistake because of arrogance and greed?

62429224
>>62429006
your so-called "correct metric" is a quaint simplification—a sanitized, sugar-coated version of a much darker, more chaotic reality. While education and freedom are indeed influential factors, they are merely the tip of the iceberg in the vast, tumultuous ocean of societal transformation. The true cosmic law, etched into the very fabric of the universe, is that as nations ascend the ladder of prosperity, the birth rate inexorably declines—an unstoppable force of nature, This isn't a linear equation nor a moral lesson; it's an intricate tapestry woven with threads of power, rebellion, and the unquenchable human desire for sovereignty. Women aren't just seeking education—they're igniting a volcanic eruption of empowerment, tearing down the old order with volcanic fury. Feminism isn't merely about equality; it's a cataclysmic storm reshaping the societal landscape, smashing patriarchal chains, and unleashing a new chaos of independence. Campaigns against traditional notions of motherhood, reproductive control, and societal submission are not mere policies—they're the weapons in a war for the soul of civilization, forging a new hierarchy where motherhood becomes a battlefield, and societal control morphs into a weapon of chaos. The ancient code isn't about mere birthrates; who controls the narrative, the body, the future. Men blind to the cosmic dance—unable to decipher the sacred formula. The age-old dance—man seeking stability, woman seeking freedom—is orchestrated by the universe itself, a chaotic symphony of power, desire, and sovereignty. This isn't a simple matter of education or freedom—it's a war for the very essence of human dominance, a clandestine game of rent and barter in the marketplace of intimacy, fueling a rise of new feudalism and sovereignty is traded like currency. The new world order; it's a battlefield of chaos and control, where human sovereignty is the ultimate prize—cosmic contest where only the fiercest survive.

62429251
techlead is still rich. he is faking being poor/broke so nobody wrench attacks him like he mentioned in the video.

62429286
>>62425420
If you can't make it in a first world nation then you never made it.

Fleeing to thirdie-ville because you can't handle hard mode is gay. Just win more.

62430416
>>62425420
Yep pretty much. You don't even need to go to the third world I spend about half the year in europe and its very easy to hook up when you're making very literally 5-10x as much as the average working man in that country.

62432602
>>62426867
Marrying Asian qts and cooming in them, no pullsies, ensures there will be a huwito race. Simping for white roasties and dying alone is genociding them.

62433063
18yo, woman held you hostage to date her
20yo, woman held you hostage to marry her
22yo, woman held you hostage to have her kids
23yo, woman held you hostage to buy a house and a lambo car with the 500k high paying job
30yo, woman held you hostage to run away with your white child
32yo, woman held you hostage to give her half of your pay check
35yo, woman held you hostage to divorce rape
40yo, woman held you hostage to cheat on you while having intimate relationships with another gigachad
43yo, woman held you hostage you hostage to no more children after this age.
45yo
47yo
50yo
54yo
60yo
62yo
67yo
70yo
…
80yo
…
90yo
…

62433175
>>62432602
Only Japanese count…anything else is a cope

62433180
>>62433063
fight club told us this in 1999

62433196
>>62433180
And in a few years woman become barren and washed up, you could still get to know more of her female friends after knwoing her by 20s, but by 30s she become totally invisible and you would not get to know more of woman from her because there is none left.

62433199
>>62425420
I miss him insulting me and telling me I am a worthless loser for wanting to code

62433238
>>62430416
Can you go into a little bit more detail about your favorite country in Europe for this and how you actually meet the women? genuine question

62433325
After age 43, she continued to manipulate your decisions about your health and body, insisting on procedures or treatments you didn’t want. After age 45, she held you hostage emotionally, threatening to withdraw love and support if you didn’t comply with her demands. After age 50, she manipulated your retirement plans, forcing you to work longer or retire earlier against your wishes. After age 55, she controlled your social interactions, forbidding you from reconnecting with old friends or family members.

After age 60, she exerted pressure to change your will and estate plans, ensuring she inherits everything regardless of your true wishes. After age 65, she manipulated your healthcare decisions, dictating treatments and medications for her benefit. After age 70, she threatened to cut you off financially or emotionally if you didn’t do as she demanded. After age 75, she kept you hostage in the relationship through guilt and fear, refusing to let you live your own life or find happiness elsewhere.

After age 80, she exerted control over your daily routines, dictating your activities and social outings. After age 85, she manipulated your perceptions of reality, making you doubt your memory or judgment. After age 90, she continued her emotional hostage tactics, insisting that her needs and desires came before yours, even as you aged and became more vulnerable.

62433374
>>62425420
id rather die a virgin than have sex with anything other than a white woman

62433382
>>62425500
Brown penis sleeves turn into quivering slugs when they gaze upon glorious white men
